title: Thea fluviatilis
intro: Thea fluviatilis, also known as the river tea plant, is a species of camellia native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Thea fluviatilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Thea fluviatilis include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used to promote general health and well-being.
taxonomy: Thea fluviatilis belongs to the family of Theaceae, which comprises several species of flowering plants commonly known as tea plants.
